text.title: At-Scale Research Assembly Line
text.summary: The six-stage pipeline for AI-augmented research operations and the platform capabilities that support each stage.

rule.statement: Structure the research pipeline as: recruit → moderate (human or unmoderated) → transcribe → tag → cluster → report → publish. Agents own transcription, tagging, clustering, and report drafting; humans own study design, ethics, interpretation, and stakeholder framing.
rule.rationale: Mixing agent and human responsibilities without clear ownership lets errors propagate through all downstream stages silently.

rule.statement: Use a research repository (Dovetail, Marvin, EnjoyHQ, Notably) as the single source of truth; have agents push artifacts with stable IDs. Track a decisions-ledger linking tagged insights to shipped product changes — the ratio is the actual ROI of scaling.
rule.rationale: Scaling research without a repository strategy produces archived reports nobody reads; the decisions-ledger proves that insights changed behavior.

rule.statement: Run a human verifier subagent that samples 10% of agent-tagged segments per study and gates publication on agreement rate of 90% or above.
rule.rationale: AI auto-tagging accuracy varies by domain; sentiment models trained on product reviews mis-tag user-research segments with sarcasm, code-switching, and non-English transcripts.

section.title: Platform capabilities
section.p: Platform tiers by team size: small teams benefit from built-in automation and AI analysis in tools like Maze and Sprig; large teams need scalable panels (Userlytics 2M+, UserTesting), collaboration across squads, and adaptable workflows with repository integration.

rule.statement: Stratify panels by role, geography, and tenure; report on stratification balance per study. "n=200 random" is a misleading scale claim without stratification disclosure.
rule.rationale: Large panels are not high-quality panels; professional respondents and geographic clustering bias results.

rule.statement: Cap unmoderated panel reliance at 60-70% of the research mix; complement with moderated and longitudinal studies for depth.
rule.rationale: Unmoderated testing cannot capture motivations, context, or unexpected paths that a moderator would pursue.

rule.statement: Transcribe in the source language, translate after tagging — never the reverse.
rule.rationale: Translation before tagging loses tag-relevant nuance; code-switching and cultural idioms that carry emotional weight in the original disappear in translation.
